channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS. Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he will change the name of the Gulf of Mexico
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says that he would move to try to rename the Gulf of Mexico to the “Gulf of America,” a name he said has a “beautiful ring to it.”.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran's nuclear program is nearing 'the point of no return,' France's Macron says
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is warning that Iran's nuclear program is approaching the "point of no return," calling it a top "security challenge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US determines Sudan paramilitary committed genocide, sanctions leader
item.description: The Biden administration on Tuesday sanctioned the top general of Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and determined the paramilitary group has committed genocide in the country, as well as crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing. While the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Denmark stresses Greenland not for sale after Trump Jr. arrives on island
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen stressed that Greenland is not for sale after President-elect Trump’s oldest son, Donald Trump Jr., arrived on the Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Kim Jong Un’s big guns spotted on Russian front lines: report
item.description: A North Korean howitzer has been reported on Russia's front lines following November sightings of a shipment of two M1989 Koksan self-propelled big guns as Pyongyang entered the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump won't rule out using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump repeated his warnings that all hell will break loose in the Middle East if hostages kidnapped from Israel and held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk helped Trump win. Now he's looking at Europe, and many politicians are alarmed
item.description: Fresh from pouring his money and energies into helping Donald Trump win re-election, Elon Musk has trained his sights on Europe.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France marks 10 years since deadly Charlie Hebdo attack
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A decade after Islamic extremist gunmen stormed the offices of the satirical newspaper Charlie Hebdo in a deadly assault that shook France to its core and ignited a global outcry in defense of freedom of speech, the nation paused on Tuesday to honor the victims and renew its resolve to
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bangladesh's ailing former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia to undergo medical treatment in London
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s ailing former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia was traveling to London on Tuesday for medical treatment, her aides said, leaving behind a nation grappling with uncertainty over its political future after her archrival, former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, was ousted i
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Controversial founder of French far right, Jean-Marie Le Pen, dies aged 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, who founded France's far-right National Front party, died at 96 years old. Le Pen was a controversial and confrontational figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. arrives in Greenland amid father's interest in acquiring island
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. has arrived in Greenland amid his father’s interest in acquiring the island. “Don Jr. and my Reps landing in Greenland. The reception has been great. They, and the Free World, need safety, security, strength, and PEACE! This is a deal that must happen. MAGA.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Senegal and Chad say ousting of French troops was their sovereign decision
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Senegalese and Chadian leaders said that the ousting of French troops from their territories was their sovereign decision, rejecting remarks from French President Emmanuel Macron that suggested otherwise.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and BABACAR DIONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who was Dietrich Bonhoeffer? An AP Explainer about the anti-Nazi pastor
item.description: Who was Dietrich Bonhoeffer? Dietrich Bonhoeffer was a Lutheran pastor and theologian from Germany executed by the Nazis in 1945 at age 39 for his role in a resistance movement that included attempts to assassinate German leader Adolf Hitler.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What would Bonhoeffer do? Anti-Nazi pastor's legacy claimed, debated across political spectrum
item.description: Shortly before he was hanged by the Nazis in 1945 at age 39, Dietrich Bonhoeffer reportedly told a fellow prisoner: “This is the end — for me the beginning of life.” It was – more than he knew.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why more frequent cold blasts could be coming from global warming
item.description: Frigid air that normally stays trapped in the Arctic has escaped, plunging deep into the United States for an extended visit that is expected to provoke teeth-chattering but not be record-shattering.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Severe flood warning issued as the UK faces more weather disruptions
item.description: Serious flooding swamped parts of England and snow forced three airports to close temporarily as wet and icy weather combined to extend travel chaos that has plagued the U.K. since the start of the year.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy stands trial over alleged campaign funding by Libya's Gadhafi
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy went on trial Monday over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Nigel Farage responds after Elon Musk declares he 'doesn't have what it takes' to lead Reform UK Party
item.description: Elon Musk targeted Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage, saying he "doesn't have what it takes" and the party "needs a new leader.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German Christmas market attack victim dies at hospital weeks later, increasing death toll to 6
item.description: A woman who was injured during the Dec. 20 attack at a German Christmas market died at a hospital on Monday, increasing the death toll to six.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau to announce resignation as early as Monday: report
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, facing dwindling popularity in his country, is expected to resign this week, according to the Globe and Mail. He took office in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli PM office denies reports that Hamas forwarded list of hostages to release in event of deal
item.description: Israel denied reports that Hamas forwarded a list of 34 hostages it could release in exchange for a cease-fire Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9
